title: tui /cat truncates at raw byte offset — may split UTF-8 character
description: tui/src/event.rs:1269-1274: /cat reads a file and truncates at byte offset 4096 (&bytes[..4096]) before calling String::from_utf8_lossy. This is a raw byte cut that may split a multi-byte UTF-8 sequence. from_utf8_lossy then replaces the partial byte with U+FFFD, leaking a garbled character at the boundary. DESIGN.md invariant 16 requires truncation to use char_indices() to find a safe boundary. Fix: use String::from_utf8_lossy(&bytes) then truncate via char_indices() at a safe boundary, matching the same pattern used elsewhere.
